title: PySandbox Policy Manifest
description: Authoritative capability manifest (`pysandbox.policy.json`) for the pysandbox-agent VS Code extension and its companion obo-broker service. Governs which pre-approved external API operations agent-authored Python may invoke through the Identity Broker. The capabilities map IS the authorisation taxonomy: each entry names a provider plugin plus the host/path/method allowlist the broker enforces. Derived from docs/design/sandbox and docs/design/obo-broker (revision 0.6.1); supersedes the pre-pivot schema in the retired design.md.

capabilities.description: Map of capability id -> capability definition. The id is the value referenced in run().requestedCapabilities and is the stable taxonomy key.

propertyNames.description: Dotted lowercase capability id, e.g. github.repo.read or internal.tickets.

kind.description: Capability kind (ADR-034). `rest` (the default, also when omitted) mediates a REST/HTTP API by host + pathAllow + methods. `mcp` mediates a downstream MCP server over HTTP/SSE transport by serverUrl + toolAllow. The kind selects which allowlist fields are required and which are forbidden (see allOf). A capability omitting `kind` is `rest`, so manifests written before ADR-034 are unchanged.

provider.description: providerId of an in-tree, security-reviewed Provider Plugin (obo-broker ADR-009/ADR-017). The provider is selected per capability by the Provider Registry. Provider plugins are pluggable and compiled in — never dynamically loaded; there is no default provider. The reference baseline is 'rfc8693' (generic RFC 8693 token exchange against the configured IdP — Okta, Keycloak, Entra's standards endpoint, or any RFC 8693 authorization server; requires `audience`). Peer in-tree plugins extend this set for non-standard cases (e.g. 'github' OAuth, an 'entra' OBO-dialect plugin, or a header-injection access-gateway variant such as 'okta-access-gateway'). An unknown provider fails closed at load.

scopes.description: Advisory only: the issued token may carry broader scopes than listed, and per-call downscoping is not generally available. host + pathAllow + methods are the ENFORCED controls. Do not rely on scopes as a security boundary.

host.description: Exactly one allowlisted host. The broker pins outbound calls to this host, does not auto-follow cross-origin redirects, and never re-sends Authorization across a host boundary. Wildcards are not permitted — one host per capability.

audience.description: Downstream API audience for the on-behalf-of / token-exchange flow. REQUIRED for token-exchange providers (provider=rfc8693 and equivalents); omit for non-exchange providers such as github. The broker additionally validates this rule per-provider at load, because the pluggable provider set cannot be fully enumerated in the schema.

pathAllow.description: Anchored regexes matched against the CANONICALISED path (URL-decoded; '.' and '..' resolved; duplicate slashes collapsed), excluding the query string. A path still containing '..' after canonicalisation is rejected before matching. Anchor every pattern (^...$ or ^.../.*) — an unanchored pattern is a privilege-escalation hazard.

methods.description: HTTP methods the broker permits for this capability. The pysandbox_sdk currently exposes get/post/patch/delete; PUT is accepted in policy for forward compatibility. REST kind only — forbidden on an `mcp` capability.

serverUrl.description: MCP kind only (ADR-034): the single allowlisted downstream MCP server origin, reached over HTTP/SSE transport. The broker pins each JSON-RPC `tools/call` to this origin, does not auto-follow cross-origin redirects, and never re-sends the credential across a host boundary — the `host` rules of a REST capability, applied to the MCP transport. HTTPS only; the sole exception is a `http://127.0.0.1[:port]` loopback origin under the ADR-032 dev opt-in (`localhost` and any DNS name do not qualify). stdio transport is not expressible (threat-model RR-10).

toolAllow.description: MCP kind only (ADR-034): the exact downstream tool names the agent may call via `mcp.<capability-id>.call_tool(name, ...)`. A tool the server advertises through `tools/list` but absent from `toolAllow` is unreachable, fail-closed — the server's advertised list is never the authorisation surface. The allowlist is over tool names only; per-argument constraints are deferred (a future field extension, no schema break).

requireStepUpAuth.description: When true, this capability requires step-up authentication: the broker (obo-broker ADR-014) demands an id_token whose `acr` claim is in the configured allowlist and otherwise returns an RFC 9470 401 challenge; the extension performs an IdP step-up and retries once (ADR-015). The step-up signal is never caller-supplied. Recommended true for write capabilities (POST/PATCH/PUT/DELETE on sensitive hosts).

authMode.description: How the broker authenticates the downstream call. `exchange` (default) routes via the obo-broker token exchange; `passthrough` forwards the user's OIDC access token (admin-signed only); `none` sends no credential (server-mode public endpoint, ADR-037); `api_key` applies a per-capability static key (ADR-036, requires secretRef + keyPlacement). `none` and `api_key` need no provider/audience/scopes.

allowWrite.description: Server-mode write gate (ADR-039): a capability is read-only (GET only) unless this is true; honoured only in an administrator-signed manifest. An unsafe method (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) without allowWrite is rejected fail-closed at load (CFG_INVALID).

secretRef.description: api_key mode (ADR-036): the SecretResolver reference for this capability's key — a file path under FileSecretResolver. Required for authMode=api_key; forbidden otherwise. The key file is read once at startup, never logged, never returned to the guest.

keyPlacement.description: api_key mode (ADR-036): how the resolved key is attached to the outbound request. Required for authMode=api_key; forbidden otherwise.

allOf.$comment: Capability kind (ADR-034): mcp uses serverUrl + toolAllow and forbids host/pathAllow/methods; rest (incl. omitted kind) uses host/pathAllow/methods and forbids serverUrl/toolAllow.
Token-exchange providers require an audience. One clause per token-exchange provider; extend as plugins are added.
provider + scopes are required for exchange/passthrough only; `none` (ADR-037) and `api_key` (ADR-036) need neither. Absent authMode defaults to `exchange`.
Step-up is not applicable to a non-OIDC capability (ADR-039).
Write gate (ADR-039): without allowWrite=true, only the safe method GET is permitted.
secretRef/keyPlacement are forbidden unless authMode is api_key (ADR-036).

requireUserConsentPerSession.description: When true, first use of each capability in a workspace session prompts the user for consent (cached in memory, not persisted).

maxCallsPerRun.description: Hard cap on outbound broker calls within a single run. Over-budget calls return a 429-shaped error.

maxCallsPerSession.description: Hard cap on outbound broker calls across all runs in a workspace session. MUST be >= maxCallsPerRun (enforced by the broker at load, not by the schema).

responseMaxBytes.description: Maximum response body size returned to the guest; larger responses are truncated with a flag.

debug.description: Developer mode: when true, request/response bodies may be logged. MUST be false in enterprise policy (the broker rejects debug=true under real-credential mode).

siemExport.description: Audit export configuration (obo-broker ADR-016). When the deployment uses real (non-mock) credentials, a reachable tamper-evident sink is REQUIRED and checked fail-closed at startup; with no reachable sink the deployment runs mock / non-sensitive-credentials-only. The exported stream is the authoritative audit record; the local .jsonl log is non-authoritative. NOTE: the precise field set is a documented assumption pending /spec confirmation.

enabled.description: Whether audit entries are exported to a SIEM/OTLP sink.

otlpEndpoint.description: OTLP endpoint URL for the audit export. Required (and must be reachable) when enabled is true and the deployment uses real credentials.
